contract.name: v2/autocomplete/location.md
contract.description: Returns case-insensitive autocomplete suggestions for locations grouped by category based on the provided search text.

search_text.description: Text prefix used to match location records; comparison is case-insensitive.
search_text.constraints: Required. Must be a string with length >= 1. Leading/trailing whitespace is ignored for matching, but whitespace-only strings still pass validation and return broad results. Non-string values (numbers, null, booleans) are rejected with 400 errors.

limit.description: Maximum number of matches returned per populated category.
limit.constraints: Optional. Default 5. Accepts integers 1-20 inclusive; digit-only strings (with optional surrounding whitespace) are coerced. Floats, non-numeric strings, null, and booleans return 400. Values above 20 return 422. Values less than 1 (including zero and negatives) trigger a 503 with no JSON body.

count.description: Sum of all suggestion counts across the arrays present in results. Always provided on success and zero when no matches exist.

results.description: Container for per-category suggestion arrays. Categories are omitted when empty.

states.description: Each entry includes state_name and country_name; state abbreviations are not returned.

cities.description: Each entry includes city_name and country_name. U.S. cities include state_name, while foreign cities omit the property entirely.

counties.description: Each entry includes county_fips, county_name, state_name, and country_name.

zip_codes.description: Each entry includes zip_code (string, preserving leading zeros), state_name, and country_name.

districts_original.description: Each entry includes original_cd, state_name, and country_name for historical congressional districts.

districts_current.description: Each entry includes current_cd, state_name, and country_name for current congressional districts.

messages.description: Array of informational strings. Observed responses return a single empty string ("").

quirks: limit must resolve to an integer between 1 and 20; invalid types return 400, values above 20 return 422, and values below 1 yield a bare 503 response.
count is always present on success and equals the total number of returned suggestions across all categories.
messages has only been observed as [''] even when categories are empty or truncated.
Result categories are omitted when empty and string values are uppercased in responses.
Foreign city results omit state_name entirely, while U.S. cities include it.
ZIP code matches return zip_code as a string to preserve leading zeros.
Leading and trailing whitespace in search_text is ignored for matching, but whitespace-only inputs still succeed and return broad results.
risks: Requests with limit <=0 return HTTP 503 without a JSON payload, so callers must validate limit before sending.
Error responses vary: 400/422 payloads include only a detail string, while 503 responses have no body, requiring defensive handling.
Foreign city records omit state_name and whitespace-only search terms broaden results, so clients should normalize input and handle missing fields.
gaps: No observed scenario produced non-empty guidance in messages; downstream meaning of populated messages remains unknown.

probes.notes: Baseline success with default limit populating multiple categories.
Digit-only string limit coerced to integer and applied per category.
Validation rejects limits greater than 20 with a 422 detail message.
Limit values below 1 trigger 503 without a JSON payload.
search_text is required when omitted entirely.
Whitespace-only search_text passes validation and returns broad results.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'limit'. '2.5' is not a valid type (integer)"}

probes.notes: Non-integer numeric values are rejected with a 400 detail message.
District-formatted queries populate both districts_original and districts_current arrays.

mismatches: Docs define ZipCodeMatch.zip_code as numeric, but production responses return strings such as '20500'.
Docs require CityMatch.state_name (nullable), yet foreign city results omit the property entirely.
Docs omit the top-level count field that is present on every successful response.
gaps: No observed scenario produced non-empty guidance in messages; downstream meaning of populated messages remains unknown.
risks: Requests with limit <=0 return HTTP 503 without a JSON payload, so callers must validate limit before sending.
Error responses vary: 400/422 payloads include only a detail string, while 503 responses have no body, requiring defensive handling.
Foreign city records omit state_name and whitespace-only search terms broaden results, so clients should normalize input and handle missing fields.
